label: Data Management Capabilities
canonical_statement: EZT Designer supports Excel/CSV data import for territory analysis and bulk CSV-based user management. Both are manual upload workflows — there is no automated sync from external file sources.

label: Integration Capabilities
canonical_statement: EZT Designer integrates with Dynamics 365, SQL Azure, Power Platform/Dataverse, Power BI, and REST API endpoints. CRM sync (D365/Territory Sync plugin) and SQL connectivity require Enterprise edition.

label: Routing & Scheduling Capabilities
canonical_statement: EZT Designer includes service call scheduling (visit frequency optimization) and bulk route optimization (multi-stop routes at scale). These are planning-time tools — not real-time dispatch or live GPS tracking.

label: Territory Design Capabilities
canonical_statement: EZT Designer builds territories from geographic units (ZIP codes, counties, states, countries) or named accounts, with drag-and-drop realignment and capacity modeling. It is a planning tool — not a live tracking or field execution system.

label: Capabilities (Index)
canonical_statement: This file is a navigation index. For retrieval, use the focused capability files below.

label: Deployment
canonical_statement: EasyTerritory Designer is a web application built on Azure Maps. It runs in a browser — no desktop software to install.

label: Competitive Advantages and Weaknesses
canonical_statement: Advantages: Microsoft ecosystem depth, Power BI Territory Visual, MapPoint migration path, flexible deployment, REST API, all-in-one suite, SOC 2 compliance. Weaknesses: limited non-Microsoft CRM, no native mobile app, smaller market presence, dated UI, no Google Maps, international geocoding.

label: Key Competitors
canonical_statement: Tier 1 direct competitors: eSpatial, AlignMix, Maptive, Maptitude. Tier 2 field sales: Salesforce Maps, SPOTIO, Badger Maps. Tier 3 enterprise: Precisely Spectrum, Zilliant, Esri/ArcGIS.

label: Market Landscape
canonical_statement: EasyTerritory Designer competes in the sales territory mapping and management category. The market spans from lightweight field-sales route planners to enterprise-grade geospatial territory optimization platforms. Occupies a distinctive niche: the Microsoft ecosystem territory management platform and leading Microsoft MapPoint replacement.

label: Catalog, UI & Power BI Limitations
canonical_statement: EasyTerritory catalog and infrastructure constraints, UI characteristics, deployment considerations, and Power BI integration limitations.

label: CRM, GPS Tracking, Geocoding & Rendering Limitations
canonical_statement: CRM integration depth, geocoding limitations, and client-side rendering scale constraints. Salesforce integration is basic (SSO only, no native component). Client-rendered polygon layers degrade above ~5,000 polygons. Freehand/digitized territories ARE supported for drawing, but may not export reliably to Power BI — use catalog boundary-based territories for reliable Power BI export. For features

label: Deployment, Power BI, Authentication & Poor-Fit Limitations
canonical_statement: Deployment, infrastructure, Power BI integration, authentication limitations, and scenarios where EasyTerritory is not the best fit.

label: Features EasyTerritory Does NOT Support
canonical_statement: Can EasyTerritory track field reps' GPS locations in real time? No. Can it do GIS spatial analysis? No. Is there a HubSpot integration or Google Maps option? No. Does it have a mobile app? No. EasyTerritory Designer is a territory planning and alignment tool. This file covers the features and capabilities that customers frequently ask about that EZT does not provide — including real-time GPS track

label: Pricing
canonical_statement: Quote-based pricing (no self-service, no free tier). Two editions: Standard (CSV/Excel, 6 hrs/yr support) and Enterprise (adds CRM integration, SQL Server, 16 hrs/yr support, security reviews). Enterprise required for Dynamics 365, Salesforce, or any CRM integration. Do NOT quote specific dollar amounts.

label: Security
canonical_statement: EasyTerritory takes security seriously. This page provides a high-level overview. For detailed security assessments, Enterprise edition customers can request security questionnaire reviews.

label: Azure AD Authentication in EZT Designer
canonical_statement: Microsoft Entra ID / Azure AD integration using MSAL. Supports popup or redirect, silent token refresh, and On-Behalf-Of token exchange for Dynamics 365 CRM.

label: Forms Authentication in EZT Designer
canonical_statement: The default built-in username/password authentication mode. Supports self-service password changes/recovery, force password change flag, keep-alive via mouse activity, and session TTL management.

label: OpenID Connect Authentication in EZT Designer
canonical_statement: Standards-based OIDC federation (Okta, ADFS, PingFederate, Salesforce, etc). Supports silent renew via iframe, special handling for Salesforce and ADFS, and RP-initiated logout.

label: Authentication in EZT Designer
canonical_statement: Three mutually exclusive auth modes: Forms (built-in username/password), Azure Active Directory (AAD via MSAL), and OpenID Connect (OIDC). Mode is set server-side — users cannot switch at runtime. SAML is NOT supported.

label: Capacity Planning
canonical_statement: Capacity planning models workload distribution by combining data-grid query results with a scheduling engine to produce per-territory day-route groupings. Requires a point data layer with a stop-delay column (visit time in hours per record). Output is summary statistics only — it does NOT draw territory shapes or modify assignments.

label: Classification — Group-By-Markup, Visualizations & Overrides
canonical_statement: Group-By-Markup (GBM) is a special classification mode where point features are colored based on which drawn polygon they fall inside — assigning the polygon's "tag" (name/group combination) to the points. This is one of the most powerful classification features in EZT.

label: Classification — Overview, Break Types & Symbols
canonical_statement: Classification assigns colors and symbols to map features based on data values. Prerequisite: columns must have the classify flag set in Project Layer Config before they appear in classification dropdowns. Supports 5 break types: Equal Interval, Quantile, Natural Breaks (Jenks), Standard Deviation, and Manual. Use Auto-classify to generate classes.

label: Classification Prerequisites & Usage
canonical_statement: Column must have the classify flag set in Project Layer Config to be eligible. Classification is used in layer stylizers, Markup Quick Recolor, Markup from Polygon Layer, and Quick Color Set. Common issues and troubleshooting.

label: CRM Integrations - Field Mapping Layer Refresh
canonical_statement: Details field mapping for push payloads, markup tag construction, layer refresh mechanics, and common issues.

label: CRM Integrations - Overview
canonical_statement: Dynamics 365 has deep native integration (managed solution, workflows, EasyMap PCF, bidirectional sync). Salesforce and SugarCRM have basic integration via SSO only — no native components. HubSpot has no integration. For Salesforce-only shops, recommend Salesforce Maps instead.

label: CRM Integrations - Pull
canonical_statement: Pull (Import) fetches a CRM view (saved query/list view) as a map layer. Supports Dynamics 365, SugarCRM, Salesforce with appropriate auth. Data is refreshed automatically on project open to keep it current.

label: CRM Integrations - Push
canonical_statement: Push sends data grid results or routes back to the CRM queue. Requires isCrmWriter or isSuperAdmin role. Batches of 500 records; supports data and routes with special handling for markup tags and route metadata.

label: Data Sources Column Flags
canonical_statement: Column flags control how each column in uploaded data is used in the application (classify, filter, search, etc.). These are auto-detected or manually set during CSV/Excel upload.

label: Data Sources Join Upload
canonical_statement: The Join Upload feature lets users augment an existing layer with new columns from a second CSV file, matched on a common key column. Supports polygon-to-point conversion and in-browser joins for custom layers.

label: Data Sources Overview
canonical_statement: EasyTerritory supports multiple ways to load data into projects for visualization and territory design. Data sources range from manual CSV uploads to live CRM connections, automated ingestion pipelines, and spatial database connections. Understanding how each source is processed helps support agents guide users and diagnose issues.

label: Data Sources Change Polling
canonical_statement: EZT Designer includes a background polling mechanism that watches for data changes initiated by external systems, enabling near-real-time data synchronization.

label: EasyMap for Dynamics 365
canonical_statement: EasyMap is a PCF (Power App Component Framework) control that embeds interactive maps directly inside Dynamics 365 and Power Platform. It provides CRM data visualization, multi-stop route optimization, and travel-time rings for field users. EasyMap is a separate product from Territory Designer — it does NOT allow territory editing; it displays territories planned in Designer.

label: Geocoding: Batch and Interactive
canonical_statement: Batch geocoding occurs during CSV upload with automatic throttling based on provider rate limits. Interactive geocoding happens via the map search bar and supports POI search for Bing. Results include standardized addresses and auxiliary fields; coordinates are stored permanently in the layer dataset.

label: Geocoding: Manual and Advanced Features
canonical_statement: Users can manually drag pins to correct positions, triggering quality = "Manually Set". Adding individual records or batches from search results uses pre-filled data. Fallback providers and cache behavior ensure robustness. Results are stored permanently in layer datasets.

label: Geocoding in EZT Designer
canonical_statement: Geocoding converts text addresses to map coordinates (latitude/longitude) using Azure Maps or Bing Maps. Results are stored in the layer dataset on upload and are NOT re-geocoded on subsequent loads. Manual pin drag sets quality to "Manually Set." ZIP-code-only inputs require a country code suffix for Azure Maps to resolve correctly.

label: Ingestion Service
canonical_statement: Automates bulk CSV data loading into EasyTerritory catalog layers via SFTP. Enterprise Edition only. Works without manual UI interaction — useful for scheduled data refreshes from external systems. NOT a real-time streaming data pipeline.

label: Managed Projects — Common Support Scenarios
canonical_statement: Troubleshooting managed projects: no markup, seeing outside region, query failures, merge issues.

label: Managed Projects — Constraint Model
canonical_statement: A managed project in EasyTerritory is a parent-child project relationship where child projects inherit territories from a parent but constrain realignment — users can only move components within their assigned region, preventing cross-region contamination. Used for delegated regional editing where multiple managers each own a geographic region. Manager-constrained projects use a managing project w

label: Managed Projects — Save/Reconciliation & Derivative Projects
canonical_statement: Reconciliation ensures concurrent multi-manager editing without data loss on save. Derivative projects allow independent manager work with explicit merge-back step. Markup ownership via managerId.

label: Map Providers — Azure Maps
canonical_statement: Azure Maps (primary) for tiles, isochrones (travel-time rings), routing in EZT. Configured via azureKey. Preferred over Bing. No Google Maps.

label: Map Providers — Bing Maps
canonical_statement: Legacy fallback for routing/isochrones. Uses spoke-and-polygon algorithm for travel-time rings (no native isochrone API). Configured via bingMapsKey.

label: Map Providers — Common Issues
canonical_statement: Troubleshooting travel-time rings, labels, hotspots.

label: Map Providers — Priority, Fallback, Ring Types, Hybrid & Dynamic Features
canonical_statement: Provider selection logic, ring type support table, hybrid renderer and dynamic labels for layers.

label: Markup Operations — Export & Restore Points
canonical_statement: Export Formats (Territory Components CSV, Territory Listing CSV, Attached Data CSV) and Restore Points (snapshots of territory component assignments for revert). The Territory column in exports is what users call the "territory name" — it is stored internally as the markup tag (the pipe-delimited Group+Name value).

label: Markup Operations — Group Operations
canonical_statement: Group Color Recoloring, Aggregate Groups, Join Visible Polygon Markup, and Create Layer from Visible Polygons handle grouping and aggregation of markup objects.

label: Markup Operations — Naming and Tagging
canonical_statement: Markup names in EZT use a pipe-delimited hierarchy to encode multi-level groupings within the name itself. The Tag System, Auto-Group, Ungroup All, Find and Replace, and Quick Renumber operations manage naming and grouping of markup objects.

label: Markup Operations — Territory Operations, H-Builder & Routes
canonical_statement: Rebuilds territory markup geometries from their stored component IDs (e.g., ZIP codes). Re-queries the underlying geography layer and reconstructs the polygon shapes.

label: Plugins in EZT Designer
canonical_statement: Plugins are server-side post-processing jobs triggered automatically when a project is saved. Common uses include syncing territory changes to SQL/CRM or triggering downstream ETL. The client polls every 500ms for status. Plugins are configured per-project by administrators — end users cannot configure or install plugins through the UI.

label: Power BI Integration — CSV Hierarchy & Territory Tags
canonical_statement: The CSV hierarchy file structure uses Group columns and Territory name extracted from pipe-delimited tags. The Primary Keys column holds markup IDs for joining to geometry storage.

label: Power BI Integration — Overview & Export Process
canonical_statement: The Power BI export packages a downloadable CSV hierarchy file plus a server-side geometry push, enabling the Territory Visual for Power BI to render territory maps. Export requires territory markup built via the markup builder (not arbitrary drawn polygons) and at least one group with territories.

label: Power BI Integration — Visual Usage & Refresh
canonical_statement: The Power BI visual uses the exported CSV for tabular data and EZT geometry storage for map polygons. Geometry updates do not require CSV re-import.

label: Layer Types and Configuration in Projects
canonical_statement: Layer types (client-side, server-side, custom, CRM, WMS), their configurations, and project metadata properties in EasyTerritory projects.

label: Project Load Lifecycle and Decoration
canonical_statement: Detailed project load sequence, decoration of layers with runtime metadata, and initialization behavior by layer type.

label: Projects & Layers — Overview
canonical_statement: A project is a saved workspace containing map layers, territories (markup), and configurations. Layers can be client-side (raw data, queryable), server-side (pre-rendered tiles, limited interaction), custom (embedded CSV/CRM data), or WMS (display-only). Project state is computed via a hash to detect unsaved changes.

label: Projects & Layers — Save Lifecycle, Filters, Markup & Security
canonical_statement: When a project is saved:

label: Advanced Querying in EZT Designer
canonical_statement: Aggregation queries, spatial geometry operations, result set bounds calculation, query suspension, state guards, and URL startup query parameters.

label: Querying in EZT Designer
canonical_statement: Querying retrieves features from data layers via point-click identify, spatial shape selection, text/attribute search, or query-all. Local (cached) layers run queries in-browser; non-cached server layers make a network request per query. A single result opens a callout popup; 2+ results go to the data grid. Holding Ctrl appends results instead of replacing them.

label: Query Routing and Filters in EZT Designer
canonical_statement: Queries route through local (in-browser) or server paths depending on layer cache settings. All queries incorporate active filters. Results route based on count: single results to callouts, multiples to data grid.

label: Query Types in EZT Designer
canonical_statement: EZT Designer has several distinct query modes: point-click identify, shape/spatial queries, text/attribute search, query-all, territory ID queries, and query across all markup.

label: Rep Locator
canonical_statement: Rep Locator is an embeddable web widget (separate product from Territory Designer) that lets website visitors find their assigned sales rep, distributor, or partner by ZIP code, city, or region. It syncs from Territory Designer's published territories. Rep Locator is NOT a module inside Designer — it is a standalone embeddable map hosted by EasyTerritory.

label: REST API
canonical_statement: The EasyTerritory REST API supports territory lookup (by geography or lat/lon), nearest-neighbor point queries, and geocoding. Authentication is token-based via a Login endpoint (username/password). Territory lookup requires the Project Markup Plugin to be configured and run first — querying territories without it returns no data.

label: Routing — Turn-by-Turn Directions, Editing & Multi-Route Balancing
canonical_statement: When "Convert to Driving Route" is selected, the system calls the available mapping API.

label: Routing — Overview & TSP Optimizer
canonical_statement: EZT routing uses a genetic algorithm (TSP solver) running client-side in the browser to optimize stop order, then Azure Maps or Bing Maps for turn-by-turn directions. 32 candidate orderings, 3 quality levels (Quick/Balanced/Best), stop priority tiers (1-4). NOT a guaranteed optimal solution. Does not use Google Maps or external routing APIs for optimization.

label: Routing Stop Priorities, Constraints, Limits and Errors
canonical_statement: Stop priority system (0-4), fixed start/end enforcement, route types (one-way vs circuit), limits, and error conditions for the routing system.

label: TSP Optimizer (Genetic Algorithm Details)
canonical_statement: The Travelling Salesman Problem solver in EZT uses a client-side genetic algorithm with 32 candidate routes, specific crossover/mutation/selection strategies, and priority-enforced operations.

label: Scheduling - Algorithm & Configuration
canonical_statement: The scheduler uses bucket creation, shuffle-and-optimize (up to 1000 attempts), variance window for visit flexibility, and a weighted cost function. Drive time is geometrically estimated.

label: Scheduling - Configuration, Output & Notes
canonical_statement: Configuration options, output produced, warning conditions, differences between workload and daily partitions, and important notes.

label: Scheduling
canonical_statement: Multi-week visit scheduling that distributes stops across workdays based on visit frequency (scheduleweekfreq column required), dwell time, and drive time. Uses a shuffle-and-score engine. Requires visit frequency data — scheduling does NOT work without it.

label: Territories - Geometry, Dissolve & Editing
canonical_statement: Territory polygons are formed via a sophisticated dissolve process (deduplication, nearest-neighbor union, hole management, sliver removal). Supports vertex snapping, edge stitching, nudge and Quick Stitch during manual boundary editing.

label: Territories - Internal Representation & Merge
canonical_statement: Every territory is stored as a set of territory component IDs plus the dissolved polygon. Supports Replace vs Changes-only merge strategies when updating from data sources.

label: Territories
canonical_statement: Territories are named geographic areas built from boundary components (ZIP codes, counties, states) or point data. Build them manually or via auto-build; realign by querying components into the data grid (not drag-and-drop). Components in locked territories cannot be moved; hidden territory conflicts prompt three options (realign anyway / copy / skip). Territories are published to Power BI or cons

label: Undo / Redo System - Internals & Issues
canonical_statement: Details on how individual markup, collection snapshots, and custom layer JSON undos work, serialization, keyboard shortcuts, and common issues.

label: Undo / Redo System
canonical_statement: The undo/redo system (Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y) tracks markup edits and custom layer data changes up to a queue depth of 20 items. Bulk operations (renumber, recolor, find-and-replace) snapshot the entire markup collection before running. The queue is suspended during active drawing and bulk workflows — actions taken while suspended are NOT undoable. Switching projects clears the queue.

label: Workload Partitioning — Advanced Algorithms & Auto-Build
canonical_statement: Used by the k-means engine, auto-realign engine, and any process that needs to estimate drive time or find nearest stops within a partition.

label: Workload Partitioning — Overview & Algorithms
canonical_statement: Workload partitioning divides point locations into sets (clusters) of equal workload, determining the number of resources needed in a territory or region where locations are repeatedly visited.

label: Customer Feedback
canonical_statement: Pain points, objections, churn reasons, and feature requests from real users.

label: Customer Segments
canonical_statement: Who uses EasyTerritory Designer and how.

label: Customer Success Stories
canonical_statement: Industry-specific use cases from easyterritory.com/client-successes/. Each story describes the industry challenge and how EasyTerritory addresses it.

label: Customer Usage Patterns
canonical_statement: How customers actually use EasyTerritory Designer — based on an audit of 214 active instances.

label: EZT Designer — Eval Scorecard
canonical_statement: Tracks every experiment, what changed, and whether it helped. Updated after each eval run.

label: AGENTS.md - EZT Help Bot
canonical_statement: For EVERY user question, you MUST call memory_search FIRST before writing any response. This is not optional. Do not answer from your own knowledge. Your knowledge base contains the authoritative, accurate information about EasyTerritory Designer.

label: Significant Releases
canonical_statement: Release history with context and impact. Populated from public release notes and version references in documentation. Will be expanded as new releases ship.

label: Product Timeline
canonical_statement: Chronological event history for EasyTerritory Designer. Compiled from documentation references and public information. Dates are approximate where exact release dates are unavailable.

label: FAQ: Loading Census Bureau Data
canonical_statement: Customers frequently request demographic data layers for territory analysis — population, household counts, average income, population density, and growth rates. This data comes from the U.S. Census Bureau and must be obtained, formatted, and loaded as catalog layers.

label: General FAQ
canonical_statement: A web application for planning and optimizing sales and service territories. It lets you import data, build territories on an interactive map, analyze capacity, schedule service visits, and create optimized routes. See the overview.md for details.

label: FAQ: Geocoding Accuracy — International Addresses
canonical_statement: International addresses — particularly from countries like Japan, India, and parts of Asia — often geocode to inaccurate locations or fail entirely. This is a provider-level limitation, not an EZT issue.

label: FAQ: Power BI On-Premises Gateway — Scheduled Refresh Issues
canonical_statement: Power BI Gateway-based scheduled refreshes with the EasyTerritory Territory Visual have several known friction points.

label: FAQ: Territory Naming Convention for Power BI Drill-Down
canonical_statement: The Territory Visual for Power BI supports hierarchical drill-down: clicking a region drills into its sub-territories. This drill-down is entirely driven by the territory tag structure in EZT Designer.

label: FAQ: Querying and Exporting Data
canonical_statement: This is the standard workflow for getting a spreadsheet where each store, account, or location row has a territory name column — so you know which territory owns each record.

label: Why Won't My ZIP Codes Move During Realignment?
canonical_statement: ZIP codes that seem "stuck" or won't move during territory realignment are almost always in locked or hidden territories. Locked territory components CANNOT be moved — they are always excluded from realignment. Hidden territory components prompt three options: realign anyway, copy (overlap), or skip. Check the markup panel for locked/hidden territory status.

label: FAQ: Dealing with Territory Overlaps
canonical_statement: Territory overlaps occur when the same geographic unit (ZIP code, county, etc.) is assigned to more than one territory. There are two main causes:

label: Territory Planning — Feature Frequency & Clarifications
canonical_statement: Expert guidance on feature frequency for AI agents. Addresses when to recommend different partitioning and territory creation methods.

label: Territory Planning — Common Mistakes to Avoid
canonical_statement: Common mistakes agents should avoid when guiding users on territory planning in EasyTerritory Designer. Key issues: realignment is NOT drag-and-drop (query components into the data grid, use territory action link); column configuration requires specific navigation (gear icon → Settings → Configure Columns); don't assume unique ID columns are required; know the difference between Designer (planners

label: Territory Planning — Agent Guidance (Part 2): Performance & Response Patterns
canonical_statement: When a user wants to add a polygon boundary layer (ZIP codes, counties, etc.), always ask about the geographic scope if they don't specify it. The performance concern is rendering polygons in the browser — this applies whether the polygons are classified/colored or not.

label: FAQ: Configuring Users, Groups, and Project Access
canonical_statement: EZT Designer has three primary access levels:

label: FAQ: ZIP Code and Postal Code Issues
canonical_statement: ZIP code data issues are a regular support category. They typically fall into three buckets:

label: EZT Designer — Glossary (Data & Layer Terms)
canonical_statement: Definitions for Data & Layer terms: Client layer, Server layer, Custom layer, Catalog, Column flag, Classify flag, Shape-stats, GBM, Geocoding.

label: EZT Designer — Glossary (Partitioning & Routing)
canonical_statement: Definitions for Partitioning & Routing Terms: Simple Partitioning, Workload Partitioning, TSP, Partition, Circuit/Tour mode.

label: EZT Designer — Glossary (Project & Workflow)
canonical_statement: Definitions for Project & Workflow Terms: Rolodex, Managed project, Markup Plugin, Digitize tool, Quick Stitch, All Stats.

label: EZT Designer — Glossary (Territory & Markup)
canonical_statement: Definitions for Territory & Markup terms, including Territory, Markup, Parts layer, Dissolve, Realignment, Locked/Hidden territory, Aggregate, and Tag.

label: EZT Designer — Glossary (Upload & Data Issues)
canonical_statement: Definitions for Upload & Data Issues: upload record limit, silent truncation, double-quote parsing, pipe scrubbing.

label: Admin — Groups: List, Detail Form & Upload
canonical_statement: The Groups section allows administrators to create and manage user groups. Groups control which users have access to which projects, and can apply a data filter restricting what data those users see within assigned projects.

label: Admin — Group Reassign Dialog
canonical_statement: The Group Reassign Dialog provides a quick way to bulk-assign or unassign groups for a specific user or project, without navigating to each group individually.

label: Admin: Layer Catalog — Edit Form Advanced Sections
canonical_statement: Advanced sections of the Layer Edit Form include default symbol editor, data columns configuration with uses/classification, save footer options, and key dynamic behaviors.

label: Admin: Layer Catalog — Edit Form Properties
canonical_statement: The Layer Edit Form in the right panel contains header with layer type and identifier, basic properties, numeric properties, and behavior toggles that change dynamically based on layer type.

label: Admin: Layer Catalog — Catalog Panel & Layer Edit Form
canonical_statement: The Layer Catalog is the admin-side screen for creating, editing, and managing map layers available to all users. Left panel lists all layers in the catalog; selecting a layer opens its configuration form in the right panel.

label: Admin: Layer Catalog — Column Uses (Flags) Dialog
canonical_statement: Opened by clicking a column's Uses link in the Data Columns table. Sets which purposes/uses (flags) apply to the selected data column.

label: Admin: Layer Catalog — Exact Values Dialog
canonical_statement: Opened by clicking the ✏ icon on a column row when Class/Filter is set to "Exact". Defines a fixed list of allowed values and their display labels for a data column.

label: Admin: Layer Catalog — New Layer Dialog
canonical_statement: Opened by clicking the ⊕ (New Layer) button in the Catalog panel header. Small centered modal for choosing a layer type and initial data source before opening the full edit form.

label: Admin Panel — Log Tab
canonical_statement: Accessed by navigating to the Admin area and clicking the Log tab in the admin navigation bar. This is one of six sections in the Admin panel (alongside Projects, Catalog, Users, Groups, and Settings). The Log tab is visible and accessible to admin users only.

label: Admin Section — Projects
canonical_statement: The Projects section allows admins to browse, filter, sort, and edit all projects in the system. Two-panel layout: left panel = project list (240px), right panel = project detail form.

label: Admin Section — Navigation, Settings Tab
canonical_statement: The Admin section is accessible only to users with admin privileges. It provides six management tabs: Projects, Catalog (Layers), Users, Groups, Settings, and Log.

label: Admin Section — User Detail Form
canonical_statement: Right panel form for viewing and editing individual user details, including fields, checkboxes, password controls, and actions.

label: Admin Section — Users List
canonical_statement: The Users section allows admins to browse, add, edit, delete, and bulk-upload users. This file covers the left panel user list and search.

label: Admin Section — Upload Users
canonical_statement: Bulk user upload feature triggered from the user list panel. Supports CSV upload with specific template format and quota enforcement.

label: All Stats Dialog - Controls and Buttons
canonical_statement: Bottom bar with Classifications dropdown, color legend with analysis mode toggle, action buttons (Realign, Split, Export, Done), send-to-map functionality and dynamic loading behavior.

label: All Stats Dialog
canonical_statement: Displays a cross-tabulation of statistics for one or more markup shapes (territories, routes, or partitions). Used to compare how data metrics are distributed across markup shapes for balance analysis, territory optimization, and export. How to open and what gets included.

label: All Stats Dialog - Statistics Table
canonical_statement: The main statistics table region with three-level headers, data rows for each markup, cell color coding, and footer summary rows (total, mean, std-dev).

label: Dashboard Layout and Panels
canonical_statement: Layout of dashboard panels over the map canvas, BI-Only mode, narrow-screen panel rules.

label: Dashboard Shell, Top Bar & Startup
canonical_statement: The main application frame including persistent header, top toolbar with search, dashboard layout panels, startup workflows, keyboard shortcuts and navigation protection.

label: Dashboard Top Bar - Search Components
canonical_statement: Search mode toggles, layer selector, search box with auto-complete in the dashboard top toolbar.

label: Startup Workflows and Keyboard Shortcuts
canonical_statement: Startup scenarios for project loading, keyboard shortcuts, route guard for navigation protection, and dynamic behaviors of the shell.

label: Auto-Range Dialog - Behavior and Outcomes
canonical_statement: Action buttons, post-Done processing, class break boundary rules, and behavior notes for the Auto-Range dialog.

label: Auto-Range Dialog - Configuration Fields
canonical_statement: Details the configuration options in the Auto-Range dialog: Number of Classes, Break Type, Color Type (for colors) and Unique Symbols (for symbols).

label: Auto-Range Dialog
canonical_statement: Modal dialog for configuring how numeric data is automatically divided into classification ranges (class breaks). Triggered during auto-classification of numeric columns; controls number of classes, break type and assignment style.

label: Color Chooser Controls and Buttons
canonical_statement: Color and opacity input controls, preview swatch, validation rules, and action buttons that complete the color selection process.

label: Color Chooser Swatches
canonical_statement: A grid of color swatches organized into 10 vertical gradient columns, each blending from a dark shade at top to a bright/saturated shade at bottom. Each column contains 5 swatches (positioned top-to-bottom).

label: CSV Geocoding Process
canonical_statement: Details the background geocoding process, batching by country, handling of pre-geocoded lat/lon rows, cache behavior, quality assignment, and monthly budget tracking.

label: CSV Geocoding Progress UI
canonical_statement: The CSV Geocoding Progress Dialog shows live progress with a progress bar, stats on geocode quality, and a cancel button. Appears automatically after CSV metadata configuration.

label: CSV Geocoding Results and Completion
canonical_statement: Cancel behavior, what happens on completion (new layer creation, merge logic, handling of existing layers), geocode quality values, and "Send failed to results-grid" option.

label: CSV Metadata Configuration Dialog — Auto-Detection Logic
canonical_statement: Describes the system's automatic column role assignment based on header patterns and data scanning, plus scanning up to 1,000 rows for numeric, percentage, date, and unique value detection.

label: CSV Metadata Configuration Dialog — Column Configuration
canonical_statement: Appears after CSV upload to let users review and configure how data columns are interpreted for geocoding, set primary keys, assign roles like Full Address or Latitude, and control default behaviors.

label: CSV Metadata Configuration Dialog — Column Table & Role Values
canonical_statement: Details the main scrollable column configuration table, the special auto-generated primary key row, editable roles for each data column, and the complete list of available column roles with their meanings.

label: CSV Metadata Configuration Dialog — Bottom Controls, Validation & Behavior
canonical_statement: Location: below the scrollable column table, separated by a horizontal rule.

label: Download Activities Dialog
canonical_statement: Opened by clicking the 📥 Download icon (6th icon in header toolbar). Presents download activity types as radio-button groups. Appears as a centered modal overlay. Sections are dynamic — some only appear when the project contains partitions or routes.

label: Grid Row Editor Dialog
canonical_statement: Form dialog for editing a single record's attributes in the Results Data Grid. Available only for editable custom layers. Includes form fields with editable/read-only/required states and action buttons.

label: Grid Row Editor Dialog — Validation, Saving & Results Grid Context
canonical_statement: Validation rules, action button behavior, post-save updates, and context on how the Results Data Grid and its pager work with this editor.

label: Identify Mode
canonical_statement: Activated by clicking the ℹ️ Identify Mode icon (3rd icon in header toolbar). When active, the icon shows an orange box around it (confirmed in screenshot). Clicking on the map spatially queries the active layer (the layer with the orange underline in the legend) and displays a callout popup for the nearest feature.

label: Layers Panel Upload and Download Dialogs
canonical_statement: The Upload and Download dialogs are launched from toolbar buttons in the Layers Panel. They provide options for importing data and exporting markup, results, and territories.

label: Layers Panel Legend Section
canonical_statement: The Legend Section displays all layers in the active project. Controls layer visibility, classification toggling, and settings access. It is always present at the bottom of the Layers Panel.

label: Dashboard Layers Panel
canonical_statement: The Layers Panel is the collapsible left-side panel that is the primary workspace control hub. Contains the toolbar (top), optionally the Shapes Editor section, optionally the Power BI Tools section, and always the Legend section at the bottom. Slides in from the left edge; toggled by a vertical ellipsis (⋮) tab on its right edge.

label: iface-login-flows
canonical_statement: Login flows for Forms, AAD, and OIDC modes, plus post-login routing, session management, logout, and password management.

label: iface-login-management
canonical_statement: Password management, anonymous mode, white-labeling, common support scenarios, and related links.

label: Login & Authentication
canonical_statement: The login screen is the entry point to EZT Designer when the application is not in anonymous mode. The system supports three distinct authentication modes: Forms, AAD, and OIDC.

label: Main Workspace — Advanced Topics
canonical_statement: Keyboard shortcuts, startup behavior, answers to open questions, and detailed reference docs for the main workspace.

label: Main Workspace — Overview
canonical_statement: The primary interface of Territory Designer. A single-page application with a central map canvas surrounded by collapsible panels. This document provides the visual overview and orientation.

label: Map Canvas
canonical_statement: The map canvas fills the entire dashboard area. Rendered using the mapsjs widget. All other panels float on top of it.

label: Territory Builder (Progress) Dialog
canonical_statement: This is the processing dialog that appears while the system is actively building territory markup polygons from a list of territory part IDs. It is not interactive — the user cannot configure anything here. The system automatically opens this dialog after configuration is done in the Territory Builder Meta Dialog, and it closes itself when building is complete.

label: Territory Builder Meta Dialog - Cleanup Options
canonical_statement: Cleanup options section (remove holes, overlapping rings, report missing parts) that appears when point layer or lat/lon data is available in the project.

label: Territory Builder Meta Dialog - Column Mapping
canonical_statement: The core column mapping table that allows users to assign roles like Territory Part ID, Name, Group to columns in their pasted data, with auto-detection logic and live preview.

label: Territory Builder Meta Dialog (Overview)
canonical_statement: First step of the Territory Builder workflow — configuration dialog for mapping pasted tabular data columns to territory part IDs, names, groups and manager emails before launching the build process.

label: Markup Group Properties Dialog
canonical_statement: A small modal dialog (380px wide) for editing the visual style and name of a markup group — all territories/shapes that share a group label in the markup panel. Changes apply to every shape in the group simultaneously.

label: Markup Panel - List and Items
canonical_statement: The markup list with group rows and individual item controls for visibility, selection, editing, locking, and deletion.

label: Dashboard: Markup Panel (Overview)
canonical_statement: The Markup Panel that slides in from the right to manage drawn shapes/markup list, with header controls, search, measurements, list items, and stats for selected shapes.

label: Markup Panel - Stats Section
canonical_statement: The lower stats panel showing computed statistics for the currently selected markup shape when a visible point layer is present.

label: Mobile Shell - Active Layer, Logout & Support
canonical_statement: Active layer auto-selection logic, logout behavior, and common troubleshooting scenarios for the mobile shell.

label: Mobile Shell - Key Differences & Behaviors
canonical_statement: Differences from desktop mode, startup behavior, GPS tracking details, active layer management, project list dialog, and common support scenarios.

label: Mobile Shell (Overview)
canonical_statement: Mobile-optimized shell for EZT Designer with simplified header, GPS tracking, single-panel mode, automatic active layer selection, and streamlined project list dialog.

label: Project Layer Config Dialog — Data Columns Table
canonical_statement: The scrollable columns table in the Project Layer Config Dialog. Each row represents a data column with source name, type, label, uses/flags, and classification/filter constraints. Columns are drag-reorderable unless the layer has clusters, routes, or CRM connections.

label: Project Layer Config Dialog — Overview & Layer Settings
canonical_statement: Centered modal (820px) for configuring a layer's display properties and data columns. Opened from a project layer's settings/edit action. Operates on a copy — changes apply only on "Done." Canceling discards all edits.

label: Project Saved Info Dialog
canonical_statement: Appears automatically after a project is successfully saved — it is not manually triggered. It confirms the save was successful and provides links and identifiers that users need for sharing and Power BI integration. Appears as a centered modal overlay.

label: Push Results Dialog
canonical_statement: Collects submission metadata before pushing the current Results Data Grid or route data to a connected CRM (Customer Relationship Management) system. This dialog only appears in deployments configured with CRM integration (Dynamics 365, SugarCRM, or Salesforce).

label: Query Dialog — Execution & Behavior
canonical_statement: When Query is clicked, results flow to the Results Data Grid (single result shows a Callout instead). Options like Append, Group by markup, Create partition, and Delete markup execute conditionally. Layer switching dynamically updates field visibility.

label: Query Dialog — Overview & Form Fields
canonical_statement: Centered modal (380px) for running layer queries. Opened from the Query All icon, Results Panel, Shapes Editor, or Callout. Form fields vary by context — Delete markup, Group by markup, Append results, and Create partition options appear conditionally.

label: Re-Geocode Dialog — Save Behavior & Use Cases
canonical_statement: When Save is clicked, the point's coordinates, Full Corrected Address, and Geocode Quality are updated. Quality is set to "Manually Set" which protects the record from being overwritten during future CSV merge uploads.

label: Re-Geocode Dialog — Overview & UI
canonical_statement: Compact dialog (450px) for relocating a single geocoded point to a new address. Opened from a point's callout edit options → "Re-geocode by address." Enter a new address, click Locate to preview on map, then Save to commit. Sets Geocode Quality to "Manually Set" to protect from future merge overwrites.

label: Dashboard: Results Panel
canonical_statement: The Results Panel slides up from the bottom of the map canvas (~285px height when open). Appears automatically when query results are available. Contains a scrollable data grid of features.

label: Callout
canonical_statement: A pop-up panel anchored to a map feature (point or polygon) that shows attribute data for the selected feature. The callout is the primary way users interact with individual data records on the map.

label: Marker and Expander
canonical_statement: Marker is a simplified pop-up panel for geocoded address results. Expander is a collapsible panel wrapper used to show/hide sections of content.

label: Reusable Components
canonical_statement: These are shared UI components that appear in multiple places throughout EZT Designer. They are not standalone screens — they are embedded inside other views (the main workspace, the mobile shell, dialogs, etc.).

label: Rolodex Dialog - Layer Catalog Tab
canonical_statement: The Layer Catalog tab provides a two-column interface for selecting layers: the left lists all available catalog layers for addition; the right ("MAP") shows selected layers for the current project with drag-to-reorder and clear functionality.

label: Rolodex Dialog
canonical_statement: The Rolodex Dialog is the central hub for managing projects and map layers, opened via the Rolodex icon in the header toolbar. It features tabs for Projects and Layer Catalog with two-column layouts and drag-drop reordering.

label: Rolodex Dialog - Projects Tab
canonical_statement: The Projects tab shows a paginated list of saved projects on the left with detailed cards and special filter keywords. The right column shows a read-only draggable preview of layers for the selected project.

label: Route Partitioning — Other Partition Dialogs
canonical_statement: Dialog 2: Partition by Column and Dialog 3: Create Partitions from Neighbors.

label: Route Partitioning — Create Partitions Dialogs
canonical_statement: Partitioning divides a dataset of stops into geographic clusters (called partitions) before routing. Each partition represents one day's work, one resource's territory, or one scheduled visit set. Partitions appear on the map as spoke diagrams and in the markup list.

label: Route Partitioning — Stop Delay & Advanced Options
canonical_statement: Details the stop delay configuration and advanced options in the Create Partitions dialog including distribution, first stop, clustering, and quality.

label: Route Partitioning — Workflows & Support Notes
canonical_statement: 1. Select stops in the data grid (or load a result set). 2. Choose partition method from the interim partition menu → "Create partitions based on specified criteria." 3. Create Partitions dialog opens. Configure group name, partition count or hours, stop delay, and algorithm preferences. 4. Click Done — system sends stop locations, stop delays, and configuration to the partitioning engine. 5. Engi

label: Create Route Dialog — Configuration
canonical_statement: Opened when a user initiates route creation from the data grid, or when routing all visible partitions from the markup list.

label: Create Route Dialog — Workflow & Support Notes
canonical_statement: User selects records in the data grid (Ctrl+click for multiple, or selects a partition/result set). Number of selected stops determines which options are available (optimization requires 4+).

label: Save Project Dialog — Advanced Options & Behavior
canonical_statement: Additional form fields, dynamic behavior, permission rules, and post-save actions for the Save Project dialog.

label: Save Project Dialog
canonical_statement: Opened by clicking the 💾 Save Project icon (2nd icon in header toolbar). Projects are stored in the cloud. The dialog title changes based on context: "Save project:" or "Save as new project:".

label: Schedule Dialog - How the Schedule Is Used
canonical_statement: After the user clicks Done, the system uses the schedule configuration to compute workloads, create schedule buckets, and assign stops accordingly.

label: Schedule Dialog Overview
canonical_statement: A configuration dialog that collects scheduling parameters before the system generates either workload partitions or daily scheduled partitions from a dataset. It appears as an intermediate step before the route partition process when a scheduling workflow is triggered (e.g., using a schedule-frequency-by-week column in the data layer).

label: Schedule Dialog - Schedule Configuration
canonical_statement: All fields are in a single vertical form. Each field may be hidden depending on the calling workflow.

label: Schedule Dialog - Important Notes for Support
canonical_statement: Troubleshooting common issues with the Schedule Dialog, including disabled buttons, labeling behavior, warnings, and field visibility.

label: Send to Territory Builder Dialog ("Make Territory")
canonical_statement: A small modal dialog (380px wide) that acts as the quick-entry point for building or realigning territories directly from a selection of territory parts in the data grid. Instead of pasting CSV data into the Territory Builder, the user selects rows in the data grid and uses this dialog to turn those selected parts into a territory (or reassign them to an existing one).

label: Send to Territory Builder Dialog: UI Regions
canonical_statement: Detailed description of the UI regions and elements of the Send to Territory Builder Dialog ("Make Territory").

label: Send to Territory Builder Dialog: Workflow and Dynamic Behavior
canonical_statement: Post-dialog flow, realign workflow details, and dynamic behavior of the Send to Territory Builder Dialog.

label: Settings Sub-Panel: Aggregation & Labels
canonical_statement: Controls how point features are aggregated and displayed — as individual points, hotspot density maps, or clustered groups. Also enables dynamic map labels. Only shown when at least one of its capabilities (hotspots, dynamic labeling, or group-by markup) is available for the selected layer. Part of the Settings Panel (opened by the ⚙️ gear icon in the legend).

label: Settings Sub-Panels: Classifications
canonical_statement: Controls color and symbol classifications — rules that change a feature's color or map symbol based on a data column value. Point layers support both color and symbol classifications; polygon and polyline layers support color classification only.

label: Settings Sub-Panels: SYMBOLOGY (Default Appearance)
canonical_statement: Controls the default visual appearance of layer features — the base symbol, colors, and stroke used when no classification rules override the display.

label: Settings Sub-Panel: Filters
canonical_statement: Controls data filters — rules that restrict which features are visible on the map based on column values. Multiple filters can be active simultaneously on a layer. Part of the Settings Panel (opened by the ⚙️ gear icon in the legend).

label: Settings Sub-Panel: HOTSPOT (Legacy / Alternate)
canonical_statement: Alternate or legacy version of hotspot controls. Shares state with Aggregation panel. Part of the Settings Panel (opened by the ⚙️ gear icon in the legend).

label: Dashboard Settings Panel
canonical_statement: The Settings Panel is a second collapsible panel that slides in from the right side of the Layers Panel (left edge of the map canvas). Provides layer-specific configuration controls for the currently active project layer. Only appears for client-rendered (editable) layers.

label: Shape Properties Dialog
canonical_statement: Opens automatically when a new shape is drawn on the map (e.g., after dragging out a rectangle). Can also be launched by clicking the Properties icon (ⓘ) next to a row in the Markup panel.

label: Create Shape Dialog
canonical_statement: Opens automatically when a user initiates a buffered shape creation — typically triggered when creating a shape from a point record, from multiple selected features, or when the user requests a buffer/weld/clip operation from an existing shape via the markup panel.

label: Shapes Editor Behavior and Lifecycle
canonical_statement: Details on shape drawing, selection, node editing, snapping, styling, and the full markup lifecycle in the Shapes Editor.

label: Shapes Editor Toolbar and Drawing Tools
canonical_statement: The Shapes Editor Toolbar contains seven mutually-exclusive drawing tools for creating, selecting, and managing markup shapes on the map. Located at the top of the SHAPES section in the left panel.

label: Shape Properties Dialog - After Saving
canonical_statement: What happens after the Shape Properties dialog closes with OK or Query button.

label: Shape Properties Dialog - Managed Projects
canonical_statement: The admin-only Manager dropdown and related managed project features in the Shape Properties dialog.

label: Shape Properties Dialog
canonical_statement: The Shape Properties (tag) dialog that opens automatically when drawing new markup shapes or via the Properties icon in the Markup panel. Covers name, group, admin fields, style controls and action buttons.

label: Symbol Chooser Dialog - Available Symbols
canonical_statement: Canonical list of all symbol names used in the system and auto-classification order.

label: Symbol Chooser Dialog
canonical_statement: The Symbol Chooser modal for selecting map point symbols, display size, and zoom scaling behavior, including the full 4x6 grid of available symbols.

label: Symbol Chooser Dialog - Validation and Scale Behavior
canonical_statement: Validation rules, available symbol names list, scale behavior, and behavior notes for the Symbol Chooser Dialog.

label: Travel-Time Rings Dialog - Availability and Support
canonical_statement: Requirements for travel ring functionality and common troubleshooting scenarios.

label: Travel-Time Rings Dialog - Creation and Output
canonical_statement: How travel-time rings are computed (straight-line vs routed), multi-ring/multi-center logic, and the resulting markup shape properties.

label: Travel-Time Rings Dialog
canonical_statement: Dialog for creating travel-time rings (isochrones) around one or more points, including form fields, travel modes, and buttons.

label: Unsaved Changes Dialog
canonical_statement: A confirmation prompt that appears when the user is about to navigate away or take an action that would discard unsaved project changes. Protects users from accidentally losing work. Appears as a centered modal overlay.

label: Upload Activities Dialog
canonical_statement: Opened by clicking the 📤 Upload icon (5th icon in header toolbar). Presents four upload activity types as radio-button groups. Appears as a centered modal overlay. Title: "Choose upload activity:"

label: Upload Data Dialog & Workflows
canonical_statement: Generic file input dialog that appears after clicking OK in the Upload Activities dialog for most upload activities.

label: Utility Dialogs - Alerts & CRM
canonical_statement: Data change alerts dialog and CRM login dialog.

label: Utility Dialogs - Confirm & Choice
canonical_statement: Generic confirmation dialogs and choice selection dialogs used throughout EZT Designer.

label: Utility Dialogs - Input & Message
canonical_statement: Single input dialog and general-purpose message/informational dialog.

label: Utility Dialogs - Notes
canonical_statement: Notes dialog for viewing, adding, and deleting notes attached to data records.

label: Utility Dialogs
canonical_statement: Small, focused dialogs that handle common system tasks: authentication, confirmation, data entry, and notifications. Each appears as a centered modal overlay and closes automatically if the user navigates away.

label: Utility Dialogs - Password
canonical_statement: Dialogs for changing or recovering user passwords in EZT Designer.

label: Visualization Manager Dialog
canonical_statement: Modal dialog for managing saved visualization snapshots within a project. Launched from the ⭐ Star icon next to the project name in the Main Workspace Panel-Left > Legend section.

label: Content Changelog
canonical_statement: Append-only log of content added to the EZT Designer pack. Most recent entries first.

label: EZT Designer — CSV Upload: Join & Territory Import
canonical_statement: Join spreadsheet to map layer and importing territory JSON (GeoJSON and EZT native).

label: EZT Designer — CSV Upload: Map Locations
canonical_statement: Requirements and auto-detection for uploading map locations via CSV (address/lat-lon spreadsheets).

label: EZT Designer — CSV Upload Requirements & Column Flags
canonical_statement: CSV upload requirements, upload types, and column flags for EZT Designer.

label: EZT Designer — Export Formats & Data Type Handling
canonical_statement: Export formats and data type handling in EZT Designer.

label: EZT Designer — Export: PBI Hierarchy CSV
canonical_statement: Power BI Hierarchy CSV export ("EZT Hierarchy.csv") for integration with Territory Visual.

label: EZT Designer — Data Type Handling & Additional Exports
canonical_statement: Data type inference, number/date/percentage handling, scrubbing, and additional export formats.

label: EZT Designer — Bulk User Upload CSV
canonical_statement: Format and processing rules for CSV upload of users via Admin Panel. Covers required columns, flag columns, validation, and licensing quota enforcement.

label: EZT Designer — Geocoding Columns
canonical_statement: Details geocoding column roles/flags (geostreet, geocity, etc.), quality values, auto-detection patterns, Lat/Lon bypass logic, country grouping, and postal code fallback behavior during CSV upload and geocode operations.

label: EZT Designer — Territory Build CSV Format
canonical_statement: Describes the CSV format accepted by Territory Builder for mapping geographic units to territory names/groups. Includes column role requirements, auto-detection heuristics, name/group concatenation rules, and key gotchas.

label: Help Bot Automation Commands
canonical_statement: The Help Bot supports 8 automation commands that open panels, dialogs, and modes in the Designer UI. The bot follows a two-turn confirmation pattern: explain the workflow → offer to perform the action → user confirms → bot executes. Automation tokens are hidden from the user — they only see the bot's natural language response.

label: EZT Designer — Icon to Emoji Reference
canonical_statement: This reference maps the Font Awesome icons used in EZT Designer's UI to Unicode emoji equivalents. When describing UI elements to users, use the emoji alongside the icon name to make instructions more visual and scannable.

label: Layer Types — Column Flags
canonical_statement: Column flags ("Uses") availability by layer type (Client, Server, Grouped, WMS, Custom). Includes full availability matrix and notes on which flags require number type.

label: Layer Types — Runtime Capabilities
canonical_statement: End-user runtime capabilities available in the Settings panel for client-rendered layers. Covers classification, filtering, clustering, hotspots, labels, search, callouts, grids, and more. Organized by capability and geometry dependencies.

label: Layer Types — Quick Look-Up Summary
canonical_statement: Quick reference table answering common questions about which layer types support which features and behaviors. Keep ## Related links in the overview file.

label: Layer Types — Configuration Properties
canonical_statement: Full matrices of configuration fields available per layer type: text/identity fields, numeric/zoom/scale fields, and save/catalog options. Includes detailed notes on Bleed Ratio, Where Clause, tags, and more.

label: Layer Types — Overview & Creation
canonical_statement: Overview of the five layer types supported in EZT Designer (Client, Server, Grouped, WMS, Custom). Includes descriptions, data backing, rendering method, and creation requirements in the New Layer dialog.

label: Layer Types — Behavior Toggles & Performance
canonical_statement: Behavior toggles matrix, dependency rules, performance considerations for Client vs Server layers, Cache Local implications, server caching guidelines, zoom/scale visibility, and auto-refresh details.

label: EZT Designer — Authentication Prerequisites
canonical_statement: Details the four supported authentication modes for EZT Designer (Forms, Azure AD, OIDC/OKTA, ADFS, Salesforce) including prerequisites, configuration steps for Azure, OKTA, ADFS, session lifecycle, iframe embedding and password strength requirements.

label: EZT Designer — Map Provider Requirements
canonical_statement: Requirements and feature comparison for Azure Maps and Bing Maps providers including tiers, feature availability for travel-time rings, traffic, POI, geocoding rates and provider selection recommendations.

label: EZT Designer — CRM Integration Prerequisites
canonical_statement: CRM integration prerequisites, supported systems (Dynamics 365, Salesforce, SugarCRM), edition requirements, authentication methods, credential storage and specific configuration notes for each CRM.

label: EZT Designer — Data Infrastructure
canonical_statement: Cloud-hosted vs On-Premises data infrastructure requirements, including database, spatial layers, Ingestion Service details, and data change polling behavior.

label: EZT Designer — Network & Security Requirements
canonical_statement: Network, CORS, SSO redirect URIs, access control mechanisms, data protection details, security reviews availability and notes on query string parameters.

label: EZT Designer — Deployment Models, Browser & Mobile Requirements
canonical_statement: EZT Designer is a browser-based single-page web application (SPA) available in two deployment models.

label: EZT Designer — Pre-Sales Checklist
canonical_statement: Pre-sales checklist covering deployment model, edition selection, authentication config, map provider selection, CRM integration details and data infrastructure requirements.

label: EZT Designer — Edition Comparison
canonical_statement: Comparison of Standard vs Enterprise editions, feature matrix, boundary notes, pricing information and edition requirements.

label: EZT Designer — Geocoding Quotas and Quality
canonical_statement: Geocoding quotas by provider, what counts as a transaction, monthly budget enforcement, batch throttling, ZIP code handling, quality levels, and pre-sales checklist.

label: EZT Designer REST API — Markup, Result Sets, Notes & Simplified Layers
canonical_statement: Blob storage for markup/result sets, notes, simplified geometry, and Power BI exports.

label: EZT Designer REST API — Application Configuration & Catalog
canonical_statement: Configuration, catalog, logging, notification, and activity endpoints.

label: EZT Designer REST API — Group & Project Management
canonical_statement: Group and project management endpoints, visibility rules, and save response details.

label: EZT Designer REST API — User Management
canonical_statement: All administrative endpoints require admin-level oitoken unless noted otherwise.

label: EZT Designer REST API — Authentication
canonical_statement: Base URL structure: - Cloud: https://apps.www.easyterritory.com/{GUID}/{InstanceType}/REST/... - {GUID} = customer-specific GUID; {InstanceType} = DEV, TEST, or APP (production) - On-premises: http[s]://{Your Server}/REST/...

label: EZT Designer REST API — CRM Access Token
canonical_statement: CRM Web API access token endpoint supporting Dynamics 365, Salesforce, and SugarCRM. Separate from EZT oitoken.

label: EZT Designer REST API — Push to CRM Queue & Configuration
canonical_statement: Push submission data to CRM queue and full crmConfig reference.

label: EZT Designer REST API — CRM Views & Data
canonical_statement: Listing CRM views and fetching view data as CSV for Dynamics, Salesforce, SugarCRM.

label: EZT Designer REST API — Geocoding
canonical_statement: Geocoding API that proxies to Azure Maps or Bing Maps.

label: EZT Designer REST API — Plugins & Status
canonical_statement: Plugin availability, status polling, and plugin types.

label: EZT Designer REST API — Query String Parameters
canonical_statement: Startup URL parameters for controlling EZT Designer behavior.

label: EZT Designer REST API — Nearest Neighbor
canonical_statement: Nearest Neighbor API to find closest points from a project point layer to a given lat/lon. Requires the Project Points Plugin to be configured and executed.

label: EZT Designer REST API — Territory Lookup
canonical_statement: Territory Lookup API endpoints to retrieve territories and find which territory contains a given ZIP/postal code or lat/lon location. Requires the Project Markup Plugin to be configured and executed.

label: Application Settings Reference - Geocoding, Query & Data Grid
canonical_statement: Settings for geocoding batch limits and service endpoints, maximum query results, data grid pagination, AJAX timeouts, CSV export, and related data retrieval behaviors.

label: Application Settings Reference - Map, Authentication & Service User
canonical_statement: Settings controlling map providers/rendering, authentication modes (forms, ADAL, OIDC, anonymous), and the backend service user credentials.

label: Application Settings Reference - Territory, Routing, Classification, Project Access & UI
canonical_statement: Settings controlling territory resolution and dissolve behavior, routing constraints, classification break limits, project privacy modes, user limits, CRM integration, and miscellaneous UI chrome options.

label: API Integration — Common Mistakes
canonical_statement: The Problem: The Nearest Neighbor REST API returns a Distance value that appears orders of magnitude larger than expected (e.g., 51978 instead of ~24).

label: Authentication Migration — Common Mistakes
canonical_statement: Many customers migrated from Office 365 authentication to OAuth (or AAD) starting in 2022 after Microsoft began retiring ADAL. The standard migration steps:

label: Catalog Management — Common Mistakes
canonical_statement: The Problem: Converting a dataset with a very large number of records (200,000+) to a catalog layer can corrupt the entire catalog. The conversion process may time out or be interrupted when the user saves the project, leaving the catalog in an unrecoverable state.

label: CRM Integration — Common Mistakes
canonical_statement: The Problem: Geocode or Territory Lookup workflows fail with a deserialization error: "encountered unexpected character '<'".

label: Data Upload Issues — Common Mistakes
canonical_statement: Missing records after CSV upload? Check: (1) Upload record limit — default 50,000, silently truncates above that; (2) Double quotes — unescaped " in data silently drops rows; (3) Commas in coordinates — corrupts lat/lon and breaks the map. Also watch for missing headers, special characters in names, and ZIP3 geocoding scatter.

label: Layer Configuration — Common Mistakes
canonical_statement: The Problem: When exporting a Power BI table from an EZT project, the Part Latitude and Part Longitude columns are blank.

label: User Account Management — Common Mistakes
canonical_statement: Impact: Critical — this is the most common cause of catastrophic data loss in EZT.

label: Diagnostic: Catalog Not Loading
canonical_statement: Use this decision tree when the EZT application stalls on the loading screen, catalog layers are missing, or features that depend on catalog layers (Create Rings, territory building) are not working.

label: Diagnostic: CRM/D365 Data Not Loading
canonical_statement: Use this decision tree when users cannot load Advanced Find views, Map Records data, or any CRM-sourced data in EZT.

label: Diagnostic: Territory Lookup Not Working
canonical_statement: Use this decision tree when the Territory Lookup REST API returns empty results ([]), wrong territory assignments, or stale data.

label: Authentication Error Messages
canonical_statement: Cause: Stale authentication state cached in IIS application pools after a credential or configuration change.

label: Catalog Error Messages
canonical_statement: Full error: Client.REST.Service:Method GetCatalog: ApplicationException- Catalog is corrupted or not of expected type.

label: Geocoder Error Messages
canonical_statement: Cause: The Node.js modules for the Geocoder service are tied to a specific user profile. If that user's profile is deleted (e.g., after the employee leaves), the service can no longer start. Restarting the service or rebooting the VM does not fix it.

label: Auto-Build Territories — Execution, Review & Troubleshooting
canonical_statement: After configuration, auto-build runs a multi-phase process: k-means partitioning → auto-zone assignment → auto-realign balancing → polygon dissolve. Results appear as colored polygons in the Markup Panel. Review balance via Shape Stats or All Stats dialog. Refine by querying parts and realigning.

label: Auto-Build Territories — Setup & Configuration
canonical_statement: How to automatically create multiple balanced territories (e.g., 10 territories from ZIP codes):

label: Bulk Route Building - Multi Route and Balancing
canonical_statement: Tools for routing multiple partitions at once and workload balancing across routes in a markup group. Use Markup Workflows → "Route all visible partitions". For imbalance, use realignment operation on group of routes.

label: Bulk Route Building - Single Route
canonical_statement: How do I create optimized multi-stop driving routes for one set of stops? Select geocoded records (Ctrl+Click first for start point) → route icon → configure one-way/circuit, optimize TSP, driving options, review/edit in data grid. Supports start from different layer, manual initial order.

label: Bulk User Upload
canonical_statement: How do I create multiple users at once? As admin, prepare CSV with required columns (Name, Email, etc.) → Admin Panel → Users tab → upload CSV. Requires available licenses; passwords must meet complexity rules if no SSO. This does NOT sync from CRM or handle role inheritance automatically.

label: Workflow: Capacity Planning
canonical_statement: How do I model workload per territory/rep? Use Capacity Planning action on point layer (needs primarykey + stop delay column + grouping column) → enter average hours per day → review stats dialog for total hours per group. This auto-partitions by hours and does NOT require manual selection or special scheduling flags.

label: Capacity Planning Execution
canonical_statement: Open Capacity Planning dialog from layer, enter average hours per day, automatic partitioning by group and by hours occurs internally using stop delay column and drive time, review All Stats dialog with total estimated hours, total drive time, total stop time per group, export to CSV.

label: Create a Territory - Basic Boundary Method
canonical_statement: Open project → add boundary layer from catalog → select components (Ctrl+Click or lasso) → right-click → Make Territory → name it → save. For realignment: query components into data grid → territory action link in results panel → assign (NOT drag-and-drop).

label: Create a Territory - Data Driven Methods
canonical_statement: Build territories from data layer containing territory ID column (flagged territoryid) and optional partition ID. Supports point data or existing alignments from CRM/spreadsheet. Supports replace vs changes-only merge modes.

label: Create a Territory - Custom Drawing & Issues
canonical_statement: For non-standard boundaries, use digitize tool with snapping and edge stitching. Common problems and resolutions when creating territories.

label: Import Data - CSV
canonical_statement: Upload CSV for territories (ZIP/county alignments) or point data (accounts/leads with address or lat/lon). Use Upload icon, map columns/flags. For territories add boundary layer first. Geocoding via Azure Maps. Watch for upload limits, quoting issues.

label: Import Data - CRM, Join, GeoJSON
canonical_statement: Import from CRM views, Join upload to enrich layers, GeoJSON or EZT Markup JSON for shapes. Use Upload icon or Join icon. For Join, target layer must have primarykey. ReviewTroubleshooting for common issues like geocoding quality or key mismatches.

label: Publish Territories to Power BI
canonical_statement: Save project → Download PBI Hierarchy CSV → load into Power BI (Get Data or EasyTerritory Connector) → add Territory Visual → assign Name, Classification, and Primary Keys fields → configure EasyTerritory URL and Project ID. Freehand-drawn territories may not export correctly — use catalog boundary-based territories for reliable export.

label: Create a Visit Schedule
canonical_statement: How do I create multi-week daily visit schedules? Query stops (with scheduleweekfreq + routestopdelayhours + primarykey) → partition link → "Create daily scheduled partitions" → set weeks, workdays, optimization slider. Produces one partition per workday. This does NOT use live traffic and does NOT cap stops per day beyond daily hour cap.

label: Create a Visit Schedule Overview
canonical_statement: How to create multi-week daily visit schedules in EasyTerritory. Query stops with scheduleweekfreq, routestopdelayhours and primarykey → use "Create daily scheduled partitions" in partition dialog → configure weeks, workdays, slider and daily cap. Produces one daily partition per workday. Does not use live traffic.

label: Workload Partitions (Alternative Mode)
canonical_statement: Use workload partitioning as a precursor to daily scheduling to first determine balanced territories by total visit workload across schedule period. Multiplies stops by visit frequency, then uses geographic partitioner.

label: Simple Partitioning (Create Partitions)
canonical_statement: How do I split stops into route groups or daily sets? Select points → partition link → "Create partitions based on specified criteria" → choose Fixed count or By Hours → set Cluster/Distribute slider. No special column flags required. This is NOT Workload Partitioning (which uses scheduleweekfreq for resource planning). Most common workflow for grouping.

label: Simple Partitioning Steps
canonical_statement: Select stops. Open the Partition Dialog. Configure the partition with group name, sizing method (Fixed or By Hours), stop delay, cluster vs. distribute slider, processing quality. Click Done. Review results and route partitions.

label: Workload Partitioning Algorithm Details
canonical_statement: Technical explanation of what happens during workload partitioning: pre-analysis, k-means assignment loop, and real-time progress indicators.

label: Choosing the Right Algorithm Mode for Workload Partitioning
canonical_statement: Describes different partitioning modes available: Standard K-Means, Nearest-Neighbor, and Column-Based partitioning.

label: Create Workload Partitions
canonical_statement: How do I determine number of resources/reps needed? Query stops with scheduleweekfreq → partition link → "Create workload partitions" → set weeks and hours/week. Rarely needed; most users want Simple Partitioning instead. Does NOT require routing — produces balanced clusters for later scheduling. This is NOT the standard fixed-count partitioning.

label: Tuning, Results, and Common Issues in Workload Partitioning
canonical_statement: How to tune results, interpret them, compare partitions, and troubleshoot common problems.
